title: Fluid straightening connection unit
abstract: Various connection units are disclosed. The connection unit can be configured to straighten a flow of air, such as to reduce the distance before the flow of air becomes substantially laminar. The connection unit can include a drag reduction unit. The drag reduction unit can be configured to redirect airflow to the center of the connection unit. The connection unit can include a wake diverting component configured to lift air away from the periphery of the connection unit and/or redirect the airflow towards a radial center connection unit.
claims: Claims THE FOLLOWING IS CLAIMED: 1. A connection unit comprising: an outer duct comprising an outer wall and a passage, the duct configured to convey a flow of air through the passage; a support coupled with the outer wall; a drag reduction unit coupled with the support and positioned in the passage, the drag reduction unit comprising an upstream end and a downstream end, the upstream end configured to be positioned adjacent to a fan motor; the downstream end comprising a convex curved surface that is configured to direct a first portion of the flow of air from a periphery of the passage toward a radial center of the passage; and a wake diverting component that protrudes radially inwardly from the outer wall, the wake diverting component positioned downstream of the drag reduction unit, the wake diverting component comprising: a leading edge configured to direct a second portion of the flow of air from a periphery of the passage toward the radial center of the passage; and a trailing edge configured to direct a third portion of the flow of air toward the periphery of the connection unit. 2. The connection unit of Claim 1, wherein the outer duct comprises a hollow cylinder. 3. The connection unit of Claim 1, wherein the convex curved surface comprises a generally hemispherical surface. 4. The connection unit of Claim 1, wherein the drag reduction unit is radially centered within the connection unit. 5. The connection unit of Claim 1, further comprising a plurality of support structures each coupled to the outer wall and to the drag reduction unit. 6. The connection unit of Claim 1, wherein the wake diverting component is integral with the outer wall. 7. The connection unit of Claim 6, wherein the leading edge comprises a convex curve and the trailing edge is generally straight. 8. The connection unit of Claim 1, wherein the connection unit is formed from a first connector portion and a second connector portion that are separable from each other. 9. The connection unit of Claim 8, wherein the first connector portion and the second connector portion are configured to matingly engage. 10. A system for redirecting airflow, the system comprising: an upstream component comprising an engagement portion on a downstream end of the upstream component; a connection unit comprising: an upstream engagement portion; a drag reduction unit positioned at or near an upstream end of the connection unit; a wake diverting component protruding from an internal surface of the connection unit, the wake diverting component located downstream of the drag reduction unit; and a downstream engagement portion; a downstream component comprising an engagement portion on an upstream end of the downstream component; wherein the upstream engagement portion of the connection unit is configured to engage with the engagement portion of the upstream component, and wherein the downstream engagement portion of the connection unit is configured to engage with the engagement portion of the downstream component; and wherein the connection unit is configured to receive a flow of non-laminar air from the upstream component and to provide, within a distance of less than or equal to 12 inches downstream of the connection unit, a flow of substantially laminar air to the downstream component. 11. The system of Claim 10, wherein the drag reduction unit comprises a generally hemispherical shape. 12. The system of Claim 10, wherein the drag reduction unit is radially centered within the connection unit. 13. The system of Claim 10, wherein the wake diverting component protrudes radially inwardly from an inner surface of the connection unit. 14. The system of Claim 10, wherein the upstream component comprises a fan assembly. 15. The system of Claim 10, wherein the downstream component comprises a heater assembly. 16. A method of providing a flow of laminar air to a heat transfer component in a duct of an environmental control system, the method comprising: receiving a flow of air from a fan assembly; diverting a first portion of the airflow with a drag reducer toward a longitudinal centerline of the duct; diverting a second portion of the airflow with a wake diverter positioned downstream of the drag reducer; and providing the heat transfer component the flow of laminar air a distance of less than or equal to 8 inches downstream of the wake diverter. 17. The method of Claim 16, further comprising providing an airflow that is substantially consistent in volume across the cross-sectional area of the duct a distance of less than or equal to 12 inches downstream of the wake diverter. 18. The method of Claim 16, wherein the drag reducer comprises a trailing edge with a generally hemispherical shape, the trailing edge configured to divert a portion of airflow towards a longitudinal centerline of the duct. 19. The method of Claim 16, wherein wake diverter further comprises a leading edge and a trailing edge, wherein the leading edge is curved and the trailing edge is straight. 20. The method of Claim 16, further comprising: diverting, with the leading edge, the second portion of the airflow toward a longitudinal centerline of the duct; and diverting, with the trailing edge, a third portion of the airflow toward the periphery of the duct.
